title: Champernowne Count
description:
The $n$th Champernowne word is obtained by writing down the first $n$ positive integers and concatenating them together. For example, the 10th Champernowne word is "12345678910".

Given two positive integers $n$ and $k$, count how many of the first $n$ Champernowne words are divisible by $k$.
input: The single line of input contains two integers, $n$ $(1 \le n \le 10^5)$ and $k$ $(1 \le k \le 10^9)$.
output: Output a single integer, which is a count of the first $n$ Champernowne words divisible by $k$.
source: , ICPC, Regionals, North America, Mid-Central Regional, 2022 Mid-Central USA Regional Contest, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Pacific Northwest Regional, 2022 ICPC Pacific Northwest Region, Division 1, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Pacific Northwest Regional, 2022 ICPC Pacific Northwest Region, Division 2, ICPC, Regionals, North America, Rocky Mountain Regional, 2022 Rocky Mountain Regional Contest, xiaowuc1
